issues.current_text: the type of the set itself, used as the return type of transformation operations that preserve the set's specific type
issues.problem: The description is accurate and clear, but slightly verbose. It could be tightened for consistency with the more concise style of the surrounding `@tparam A` and `@tparam CC` descriptions. Also, `C` is constrained to `SetOps[A, CC, C]`, not strictly to a `Set` — so calling it 'the type of the set itself' is slightly imprecise (it's the self-type of the operations trait).
issues.suggestion: the self type of this set, used as the return type for transformation operations that preserve the collection's specific type

general_feedback: The single filled-in @tparam description is accurate and explains the purpose of the F-bounded type parameter `C` well. It is consistent in style with surrounding Scaladoc and uses appropriate terminology. Minor tightening is possible but not required.
